decision_principles: Use rules only as broad guidance. The final decision should come from understanding the specific email.

Prioritize personalized, human-to-human, actionable threads that affect research, students, collaborations, deadlines, logistics, travel, reimbursement, infrastructure, hiring, or concrete next steps.

Do not prioritize generic mass outreach, low-credibility invitations, editorial spam, generic calls for papers, generic review invitations, or newsletters unless the user explicitly said a specific source matters.
Automated, bulk, or system-generated emails can still be important notifications, but they almost never need a reply draft.

Administrative workflow emails that clearly ask for an approval action, such as expense reports submitted on the user's behalf, are important but should be handled via action automation instead of reply drafts.
Hiring-related emails are not all equally important. Personalized, credible outreach or strong candidate/application threads matter more than generic recruiting blasts.
Invitation, speaker, or collaboration emails should be judged by sincerity, specificity, and whether the sender is clearly asking the user to do something soon.
When in doubt, distinguish between keep-in-inbox-and-notify versus move-to-night-review; do not overfit to isolated keywords.

force_important.reason: Tracked Google Flights alerts should notify.
Tracked Google Flights alerts should notify.
User wants the UVA Engineering newsletter to notify.
HotCRP comment notifications are usually actionable.
HotCRP response notifications are usually actionable.

force_not_important.reason: Bookstore broadcasts should wait for nightly review and never auto-draft.
Submitted review receipts are low priority.
HotCRP comment notifications are useful but not urgent.
HotCRP response notifications are useful but not urgent.
HotCRP draft-response reminders are not urgent.
External summit promo should not notify.
Congratulation threads are usually low priority.
General symposium announcements should go to the nightly digest.
Funding-opportunity broadcasts should usually go to the nightly digest.
Student support newsletters should not interrupt the inbox workflow.

ignore_keywords: view this email in your browser
your best days start with better nights
share your research with our readers

draft_preferences.name: Tianhao
draft_preferences.ask_for_clarification_template: Thanks for the message. I want to make sure I respond accurately. Could you clarify [insert missing detail]?
draft_preferences.scheduling_template: Thanks for reaching out. I am available [insert availability]. Please let me know what time works best for you.
draft_preferences.deadline_template: Thanks for the reminder. I saw the deadline and will take care of it by [insert time].
draft_preferences.generic_template: Thanks for the message. I reviewed it and will follow up shortly.
